title: hackelia micrantha
intro: Hackelia micrantha, also known as small bluebell or small blue heliotrope, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae) native to the western United States and Canada. The plant contains compounds such as alkaloids and glycosides with reported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Hackelia micrantha include its potential to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as an antacid and antispasmodic for digestive issues. Hackelia micrantha can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile herb for traditional medicine and herbal remedies.
